text.title: AI Cost Attribution Schema Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in LLM cost attribution with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Optional tags — schema allows tenant_id / feature to be missing.
antipattern.symptom: 20% of bill aggregated under "unknown"; FinOps cannot identify the heavy tenants.
antipattern.root-cause: Optional fields drift to mostly-empty within a quarter.
antipattern.fix: Required fields, reject missing. Anonymous calls use anon_session_{hash}, never blank (rule r1).

antipattern.symptom: Same tenant appears under "Acme Inc.", "acme inc", "AcmeINC" — three rows.
antipattern.root-cause: Display-name used as primary key; PII leaks into cost dashboard.
antipattern.fix: Stable UUID/slug from the auth layer; display-name lookup via separate table (rule r3).

antipattern.description: Cost computed at read time against current pricing.
antipattern.symptom: Last quarter's dashboard numbers change when vendor updates pricing.
antipattern.root-cause: Single source-of-truth was current pricing, not historical snapshot.
antipattern.fix: Write-time cost + pricing_snapshot_id; never recompute on read (rule r4).

antipattern.description: Sampled attribution — 1% of calls recorded "to save space".
antipattern.symptom: Sum of computed cost does not reconcile with vendor invoice; off by 80-100x.
antipattern.root-cause: Trace sampling confused with cost-record sampling.
antipattern.fix: 100% capture for cost records; sample trace bodies separately if needed (rule r5).

antipattern.description: No daily aggregator — engineers slice the raw log directly each time.
antipattern.symptom: Dashboard queries time out; FinOps stops looking; the schema rots.
antipattern.root-cause: Aggregation step skipped because "we can run a query".
antipattern.fix: Daily SQL aggregator + pre-built dashboard with invoice reconciliation column (rule r6).
